customer_name: Pecks Farm Market II East LLC

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
A young brown and white goat was seen limping on its left front leg. It was seen to jump and climb and put weight on both
front legs normally at times during inspection. This condition has not been reported to the attending veterinarian (AV) and
isn't under treatment. Some causes for a limp can cause discomfort and a limping animal must be reported to the AV for
evaluation and treatment. Ensure to have this goat evaluated by the AV and follow the treatment plan that the AV
recommends.

The west fence line on the bison enclosure is in disrepair and is not attached very well to the supporting posts. A fence in
disrepair and not attached to posts could cause injury or not contain them. Ensure that all enclosures are maintained in
good repair to protect the animals from injury and to contain the animals.

The bison water tank had what appeared to be mud and algae in the bottom of it. Mud and algae in a water receptacle are
a sign that it is not being kept clean and sanitary. Unsanitary water receptacles can make animals sick. All water
receptacles must be kept clean and sanitary.

The duck enclosure has areas of loose wire points that ducks can touch. Also, they have a rusted barrel that has sharp
edges on the bottom that ducks can contact. Rough/sharp edges and wire points could injure the ducks. Ensure to
maintain bird enclosures free from sharp points and rusted edges.

The water receptacle for peafowl had what appeared to be mud and algae on the bottom of the receptacle. Also, the
ducks do not have a water receptacle to permit drinking of potable water (they have a pond with fresh-water flowing into
it). For their health, birds must be provided with potable water to drink. Water receptacles must be kept clean and
sanitized as often as necessary to to keep them clean and free of contamination.
This was a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class C license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection by June 23, 2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee and
must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
A young brown and white goat was seen limping on its left front leg. It was seen to jump and climb and put weight on both 
front legs normally at times during inspection. This condition has not been reported to the attending veterinarian (AV) and 
isn't under treatment. Some causes for a limp can cause discomfort and a limping animal must be reported to the AV for 
evaluation and treatment. Ensure to have this goat evaluated by the AV and follow the treatment plan that the AV 
recommends. 
3.125(a)  Repeat
   
Facilities, general. 
The west fence line on the bison enclosure is in disrepair and is not attached very well to the supporting posts. A fence in 
disrepair and not attached to posts could cause injury or not contain them. Ensure that all enclosures are maintained in 
good repair to protect the animals from injury and to contain the animals. 
3.130 
   
Watering. 
The bison water tank had what appeared to be mud and algae in the bottom of it. Mud and algae in a water receptacle are 
a sign that it is not being kept clean and sanitary. Unsanitary water receptacles can make animals sick. All water 
receptacles must be kept clean and sanitary. 
3.153(a)(1)(i)  Repeat
   
Primary enclosures. 
The duck enclosure has areas of loose wire points that ducks can touch. Also, they have a rusted barrel that has sharp 
edges on the bottom that ducks can contact. Rough/sharp edges and wire points could injure the ducks. Ensure to 
maintain bird enclosures free from sharp points and rusted edges. 
3.156  Repeat
   
Watering

The water receptacle for peafowl had what appeared to be mud and algae on the bottom of the receptacle. Also, the 
ducks do not have a water receptacle to permit drinking of potable water (they have a pond with fresh-water flowing into 
it). For their health, birds must be provided with potable water to drink. Water receptacles must be kept clean and 
sanitized as often as necessary to to keep them clean and free of contamination. 
 
This was a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class C license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection by June 23, 2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee and 
must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
